pr.title: feat(knowledge): add QuestionAnswerEngine v2 and business_knowledge bot
pr.author: NoirKomBatman

nodes.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+export PROJECT_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
+export REL_PATH_FROM_PROJECT_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-prefix)
+
+# Deployment
+export DEPLOY_RESOURCE=aws_batch_job
+export DEPLOY_DOCKER_PUBLISH="9000:9000 --publish 9001:8080"
+
+# Resource Limits
+# export CPU_UNITS=4 # Should be even multiple of 0.25
+# export MEMORY_UNITS=8096Mi # Should be whole integer wiht Mi suffix
+
+
+# Make /usr/.../go/bin available in PATH.
+if which go &>/dev/null ; then
+    export GOPATH="$(go env GOPATH)"
+    PATH_add "$(go env GOPATH)/bin"
+fi
+
+PATH_add "$(pwd)"
+PATH_add "${PROJECT_ROOT}/.bin"

@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+# Pin the base image because AWS injects botocore versions
+# see https://github.com/aio-libs/aiobotocore/issues/1321#issuecomment-2759338569
+FROM public.ecr.aws/lambda/python:3.10.2025.06.16.18
+
+# Install dependencies (parser path needs OCR + libreoffice + tesseract)
+RUN yum install -y git file glibc-devel libpng-devel gcc libjpeg-devel file-devel openssh-clients mesa-libGL poppler-utils libreoffice python-dev yum-utils && \
+  rpm -Uvh https://archives.fedoraproject.org/pub/archive/epel/7/x86_64/Packages/e/epel-release-7-14.noarch.rpm && \
+  yum update -y && \
+  yum install -y --nogpgcheck tesseract tesseract-langpack-eng && \
+  yum clean all && \
+  rm -rf /var/cache/yum
+
+COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.11.7 /uv /usr/local/bin/uv
+
+# Specified in build_lambda_v2 command in run script
+ARG lambda_name
+
+# Copy project files
+COPY python/idl /var/task/idl/
+COPY python/tenets /var/task/tenets/
+COPY python/ocean_beach /var/task/ocean_beach/
+COPY python/sdks /var/task/sdks/
+COPY "python/awslambda/${lambda_name}" "/var/task/awslambda/${lambda_name}/"
+
+WORKDIR "/var/task/awslambda/${lambda_name}"
+
+# Install into Lambda's Python environment (no venv)
+ENV UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/var/lang
+RUN uv sync --frozen --no-dev --no-install-project
+
+# `--no-install-project` skips installing the Lambda package into site-packages,
+# and the Lambda runtime only searches LAMBDA_TASK_ROOT (/var/task) for the handler -- not WORKDIR.
+# Expose the handler's parent dir via PYTHONPATH so the configured handler resolves.
+ENV PYTHONPATH=/var/task/awslambda/${lambda_name}
+
+# This is also defined in config/[dev,prod].env but hardcode it here to avoid copying
+ENV NLTK_DATA=/tmp/.cache/nltk_data
+RUN python -c "import nltk; nltk.download('punkt_tab', download_dir='/tmp/.cache/nltk_data', quiet=True)"
+RUN python -c "import nltk; nltk.download('averaged_perceptron_tagger_eng',download_dir='/tmp/.cache/nltk_data',  quiet=True)"
+
+CMD [ "aws_lambda_faq_v2_indexer.app.handler" ]

@@ -0,0 +1,169 @@
+"""FAQ 2.0 indexer Lambda — S3-event-driven entrypoint.
+
+Translates S3 ObjectCreated/Removed events on `faq_v2/{biz}/{src}.{ext}` into
+calls on `QuestionAnswerEngineV2`. Status transitions, parse, ingest, and
+delete all live in the engine; this module is the trigger adapter only.
+
+Triggers (see FAQ_V2_INFRA_PLAN.md §6, §8):
+  - ObjectCreated:Put — client uploaded via presigned URL
+  - ObjectCreated:Copy — engine.async_reindex_source did a self-copy
+  - ObjectRemoved:* — operator deleted off-API; safety-net cleanup
+
+Concurrency control:
+  - The engine's PG status CAS (`pending_upload|indexed|failed → indexing`) is
+    the sole correctness guarantee against concurrent indexer + reindex
+    callers. S3 double-fire on the same eTag re-enters the CAS and is safe by
+    design — `_ingest_with_clean_slate` deletes-then-ingests, so the second
+    pass is idempotent.
+"""
+
+import re
+import urllib.parse
+from typing import Any, cast
+
+import aiobotocore.session
+import structlog
+from ocean_beach.qa_engines.v2 import (
+    PermanentIndexerError,
+    QuestionAnswerEngineV2,
+    S3Client,
+    SourceNotFound,
+    TransientIndexerError,
+)
+from structlog.contextvars import bound_contextvars
+from tenets.identifier import ULID, AppEnv
+from tenets.tracing.aws_lambda_instrumentor import aws_lambda_instrumentor
+
+from .config import settings
+
+logger: structlog.stdlib.BoundLogger = structlog.get_logger()
+
+
+# faq_v2/{business_id}/{source_id}.{ext}
+# Tenant + source IDs are extracted from the key directly — no DB roundtrip
+# required to bind the log context.
+_KEY_RE = re.compile(
+    r"^(?P<prefix>[^/]+)/(?P<business_id>[^/]+)/(?P<source_id>[^./]+)\.(?P<ext>[^/]+)$"
+)
+
+
+def _parse_object_key(record: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, str]:
+    """Return (unicode_key, etag) from an S3 event record."""
+    encoded_key = record["s3"]["object"]["key"]
+    unicode_key = urllib.parse.unquote_plus(encoded_key)
+    etag = record["s3"]["object"].get("eTag", "")
+    return unicode_key, etag
+
+
+def _parse_business_and_source(unicode_key: str) -> tuple[ULID, ULID]:
+    """Extract (business_id, source_id) from `faq_v2/{biz}/{src}.{ext}`.
+
+    Raises PermanentIndexerError on malformed keys — these can't be recovered
+    by retrying.
+    """
+    match = _KEY_RE.match(unicode_key)
+    if match is None:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError(
+            f"key does not match faq_v2/<business>/<source>.<ext>: {unicode_key!r}"
+        )
+    expected_prefix = settings.S3.PREFIX.rstrip("/")
+    if match["prefix"] != expected_prefix:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError(
+            f"key prefix {match['prefix']!r} does not match expected {expected_prefix!r}"
+        )
+    try:
+        business_id = ULID.parse(match["business_id"])
+        source_id = ULID.parse(match["source_id"])
+    except Exception as e:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError(f"unparseable id in key {unicode_key!r}: {e}") from e
+    return business_id, source_id
+
+
+async def _process_object_created(
+    engine: QuestionAnswerEngineV2,
+    source_id: ULID,
+    business_id: ULID,
+) -> None:
+    """Drive the source through the indexer state machine. The engine handles
+    PG transitions, parse, delete-then-ingest, and the transient/permanent
+    error split."""
+    await engine.async_index_source(source_id, business_id)
+
+
+async def _process_object_removed(
+    engine: QuestionAnswerEngineV2,
+    source_id: ULID,
+    business_id: ULID,
+) -> None:
+    """Safety-net cleanup. Normal deletes go through DeleteKnowledgeSource RPC,
+    which already drops chunks/vectors. This branch only fires when an operator
+    deletes the S3 object off-API."""
+    try:
+        await engine.async_delete_source(source_id, business_id)
+    except SourceNotFound:
+        # Row already deleted via API or never existed — chunks are already
+        # gone (or never existed). Nothing to do.
+        logger.info("faq_v2_indexer.delete_source_not_found")
+
+
+@aws_lambda_instrumentor(settings)
+async def handler(
+    event: dict[str, Any],
+    context: dict[str, Any],
+    logger: structlog.stdlib.BoundLogger,
+) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    env = AppEnv.infer_from_env()
+    bucket = settings.S3.BUCKET
+
+    session = aiobotocore.session.get_session()
+    async with session.create_client("s3") as s3_client:
+        # aiobotocore's stubbed S3Client is structurally wider than the engine's
+        # narrow `S3Client` Protocol — cast at the boundary instead of leaking
+        # `Any` into the engine call site.
+        engine = QuestionAnswerEngineV2(env, s3_bucket=bucket, s3_client=cast(S3Client, s3_client))
+
+        for record in event["Records"]:
+            event_name = record["eventName"]
+            unicode_key, etag = _parse_object_key(record)
+
+            try:
+                business_id, source_id = _parse_business_and_source(unicode_key)
+            except PermanentIndexerError as e:
+                logger.warning(
+                    "faq_v2_indexer.skip_unparseable_key",
+                    key=unicode_key,
+                    event_name=event_name,
+                    error=str(e),
+                )
+                continue
+
+            with bound_contextvars(
+                business_id=str(business_id),
+                source_id=str(source_id),
+                event_name=event_name,
+                etag=etag,
+                bucket=bucket,
+                key=unicode_key,
+            ):
+                try:
+                    if "ObjectCreated" in event_name:
+                        await _process_object_created(engine, source_id, business_id)
+                    elif "ObjectRemoved" in event_name:
+                        await _process_object_removed(engine, source_id, business_id)
+                    else:
+                        logger.error("faq_v2_indexer.unknown_event")
+                        continue
+                except PermanentIndexerError:
+                    # Engine has already marked the row `failed` with the
+                    # error_message. Don't re-raise — Lambda async retries
+                    # would just re-fail identically.
+                    logger.exception("faq_v2_indexer.permanent_failure")
+                    continue
+                except TransientIndexerError:
+                    # Engine has marked the row `failed` but the underlying
+                    # error is retryable. Re-raise so Lambda async retry /
+                    # DLQ can take over.
+                    logger.exception("faq_v2_indexer.transient_failure")
+                    raise
+
+    return {"statusCode": 200}

@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+import os
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from dynaconf import Dynaconf
+from tenets.identifier import AppEnv
+
+current_dir = Path(__file__).parent.absolute()
+settings = Dynaconf(
+    envvar_prefix="LAMBDA",
+    settings_files=[os.path.join(current_dir, "./config.yml")],
+)
+
+env = AppEnv(settings.APP.ENV)

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+app:
+  env: prod
+  service: faq_v2_indexer
+  version: 1.0.0
+
+logging:
+  production: true
+  level: info
+
+trace:
+  production: true
+  log_local: false
+  host: localhost
+  port: 4317
+
+s3:
+  bucket: connectly-prod-knowledge-base
+  prefix: faq_v2/

@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@
+[build-system]
+requires = ["poetry-core"]
+build-backend = "poetry.core.masonry.api"
+
+[project]
+name = "aws-lambda-faq-v2-indexer"
+version = "0.1.0"
+description = "FAQ 2.0 unstructured-knowledge S3 indexer"
+readme = "README.md"
+requires-python = ">=3.10.4,<3.11"
+authors = [
+    {name = "Connectly", email = "eng@connectly.ai"},
+]
+dependencies = [
+    "aiobotocore>=2.13.0",
+    "dynaconf>=3.1.12,<4.0.0",
+    "gevent==24.2.1",
+    "idl",
+    "matplotlib==3.9.2",
+    "ocean_beach",
+    "onnxruntime==1.16.3",
+    "opencv-python==4.10.0.84",
+    "pdf2image==1.17.0",
+    "pdfminer.six==20231228",
+    "pdfplumber==0.11.3",
+    "pi-heif==0.18.0",
+    "pypdf>=6.7.5,<7.0.0",
+    "python-iso639==2024.4.27",
+    "tenets",
+    "unstructured.pytesseract==0.3.13",
+    "unstructured[csv,docx,epub,md,odt,org,pptx,rtf,rst,tsv,xlsx]==0.18.18",
+]
+
+[tool.uv.sources]
+idl = { path = "../../idl", editable = true }
+ocean_beach = { path = "../../ocean_beach", editable = true }
+tenets = { path = "../../tenets", editable = true }

@@ -0,0 +1,147 @@
+import asyncio
+from typing import Any, Protocol, runtime_checkable
+
+import structlog
+from ocean_beach.inferencers.prompt.section import PromptSection
+from ocean_beach.models.conversation.conversation import Conversation
+from ocean_beach.models.qa_engines.models import QuestionAnswerPair
+from pydantic import Field
+from tenets.utility_classes.pydantic import ConnectlyBaseModel
+
+logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)
+
+MIN_BUSINESS_KNOWLEDGE_QUERY_LENGTH = 5
+
+
+class BusinessKnowledgeConfig(ConnectlyBaseModel):
+    enabled: bool = True
+    max_results: int = 3
+    score_threshold: float = 0.6
+    timeout_s: float = 1.0
+
+
+class BusinessKnowledgeResult(ConnectlyBaseModel):
+    text: str
+    source_type: str
+    title: str | None = None
+    score: float | None = None
+    source_id: str | None = None
+    metadata: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
+
+
+class BusinessKnowledgeContext(ConnectlyBaseModel):
+    query: str
+    results: list[BusinessKnowledgeResult] = Field(default_factory=list)
+
+
+@runtime_checkable
+class BusinessKnowledgeRetriever(Protocol):
+    async def async_retrieve(self, query: str) -> BusinessKnowledgeContext: ...
+
+
+@runtime_checkable
+class QASearchEngineLike(Protocol):
+    async def async_search(self, query: str) -> list[QuestionAnswerPair]: ...
+
+
+class QABusinessKnowledgeRetriever:
+    def __init__(self, qa_search_engine: QASearchEngineLike):
+        self.qa_search_engine = qa_search_engine
+
+    async def async_retrieve(self, query: str) -> BusinessKnowledgeContext:
+        qa_pairs = await self.qa_search_engine.async_search(query)
+        results = [business_knowledge_result_from_qa_pair(pair) for pair in qa_pairs]
+        return BusinessKnowledgeContext(query=query, results=results)
+
+
+def business_knowledge_result_from_qa_pair(pair: QuestionAnswerPair) -> BusinessKnowledgeResult:
+    return BusinessKnowledgeResult(
+        text=f"Q: {pair.question}\nA: {pair.answer}",
+        source_type="qa_pair",
+        title=pair.question,
+        score=pair.score,
+        source_id=str(pair.id),
+        metadata={"sheet_id": str(pair.sheet_id)},
+    )
+
+
+def latest_user_text(conversation: Conversation) -> str:
+    user_event = conversation.get_last_user_message_event()
+    if user_event is None:
+        return ""
+    return user_event.get_text().strip()
+
+
+def should_retrieve_business_knowledge(query: str) -> bool:
+    return len(" ".join(query.strip().split())) >= MIN_BUSINESS_KNOWLEDGE_QUERY_LENGTH
+
+
+async def retrieve_business_knowledge_context(
+    *,
+    retriever: BusinessKnowledgeRetriever | None,
+    query: str,
+    config: BusinessKnowledgeConfig | None = None,
+    component: str,
+) -> BusinessKnowledgeContext:
+    config = config or BusinessKnowledgeConfig()
+    if not config.enabled or retriever is None or not should_retrieve_business_knowledge(query):
+        return BusinessKnowledgeContext(query=query, results=[])
+
+    try:
+        context = await asyncio.wait_for(
+            retriever.async_retrieve(query.strip()),
+            timeout=config.timeout_s or None,
+        )
+    except Exception:
+        logger.warning(
+            "business_knowledge_retrieval_failed",
+            component=component,
+            query=query,
+            exc_info=True,
+        )
+        return BusinessKnowledgeContext(query=query, results=[])
+
+    filtered = [
+        result
+        for result in context.results
+        if result.score is None or result.score >= config.score_threshold
+    ]
+    filtered.sort(key=lambda result: result.score or 0, reverse=True)
+    filtered = filtered[: config.max_results]
+    logger.info(
+        "business_knowledge_retrieved",
+        component=component,
+        query=query,
+        result_count=len(filtered),
+        source_ids=[result.source_id for result in filtered if result.source_id],
+    )
+    return BusinessKnowledgeContext(query=context.query or query, results=filtered)
+
+
+def build_business_knowledge_context_section(
+    context: BusinessKnowledgeContext,
+) -> PromptSection | None:
+    if not context.results:
+        return None
+
+    lines = [
+        "Relevant business knowledge retrieved for the customer's latest message.",
+        "Use this context only for general store, policy, process, contact, delivery, payment, "
+        "return, or other business-information questions.",
+        "Prefer SOP tools, session state, and customer-specific data for orders, cart, address, "
+        "delivery cost, coverage, stock, account, or other customer-specific facts.",
+        "If this context conflicts with tool output or grounded session data, tool output and "
+        "grounded session data win.",
+        "Do not invent store-specific facts when this context does not contain the answer.",
+        "",
+    ]
+    for i, result in enumerate(context.results, 1):
+        score = f"{result.score:.2f}" if result.score is not None else "?"
+        title = result.title or result.source_type
+        source_id = f", id: {result.source_id}" if result.source_id else ""
+        lines.append(f"Source {i} ({result.source_type}, score: {score}{source_id})")
+        lines.append(f"Title: {title}")
+        lines.append(result.text)
+        lines.append("")
+
+    return PromptSection(name="business_knowledge_context", lines=lines)

@@ -0,0 +1,259 @@
+"""FAQ 2.0 indexer — drives a single source through the status state machine.
+
+Called by the rehashed `faq_v2_indexer` lambda (FAQ_V2_INFRA_PLAN.md §6) once
+the S3 ObjectCreated lock is acquired and the row's `s3_uri` / `mime_type` /
+`file_size_bytes` have been populated.
+
+Every dependency is injected — there is no `AppEnv` lookup, no module-level
+singleton — so tests can wire fakes for repo / KB / parser / S3.
+
+Backing plan: §5.3 (indexing flow), §6.3 (transient vs permanent errors).
+"""
+
+from datetime import datetime
+from io import BytesIO
+from typing import Any, Protocol
+from urllib.parse import urlparse
+
+import structlog
+from ocean_beach.models.knowledge_bases import DocumentV1, DocumentV1AssetMetadata
+from structlog.contextvars import bound_contextvars
+from tenets.identifier import ULID
+
+from .models import (
+    KnowledgeSource,
+    KnowledgeSourceStatus,
+    PermanentIndexerError,
+    TransientIndexerError,
+    validate_file_size,
+    validate_mime_type,
+)
+
+logger: structlog.stdlib.BoundLogger = structlog.get_logger()
+
+
+# Statuses we're allowed to start indexing from. Anything else (already
+# `indexing`, `deleted`) means we no-op and let whoever owns the row finish.
+_REINDEXABLE_STATUSES: frozenset[KnowledgeSourceStatus] = frozenset(
+    {
+        KnowledgeSourceStatus.PENDING_UPLOAD,
+        KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXED,
+        KnowledgeSourceStatus.FAILED,
+    }
+)
+
+_ERROR_MESSAGE_LIMIT = 500
+
+
+# Narrow protocols so tests don't need to construct full KnowledgeBaseV2 /
+# DocumentParserV1 / aiobotocore S3 clients.
+
+
+class IndexableKnowledgeBase(Protocol):
+    async def async_ingest(self, document: DocumentV1) -> None: ...
+    async def async_delete(self, document_id: ULID, business_id: ULID) -> None: ...
+
+
+class DocumentParser(Protocol):
+    async def async_parse_document(
+        self,
+        raw_document: BytesIO,
+        metadata: DocumentV1AssetMetadata,
+    ) -> DocumentV1: ...
+
+
+class S3GetObjectClient(Protocol):
+    async def get_object(self, *, Bucket: str, Key: str) -> dict[str, Any]: ...
+
+
+class SourceRepository(Protocol):
+    async def async_get_by_id(
+        self,
+        source_id: ULID,
+        business_id: ULID,
+        *,
+        include_deleted: bool = ...,
+    ) -> KnowledgeSource | None: ...
+
+    async def async_transition(
+        self,
+        source_id: ULID,
+        *,
+        expected: KnowledgeSourceStatus,
+        new: KnowledgeSourceStatus,
+        **field_updates: Any,
+    ) -> KnowledgeSource | None: ...
+
+
+def _parse_s3_uri(s3_uri: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
+    """`s3://bucket/key/path` → (`bucket`, `key/path`)."""
+    parsed = urlparse(s3_uri)
+    if parsed.scheme != "s3" or not parsed.netloc or not parsed.path:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError(f"malformed s3_uri: {s3_uri!r}")
+    return parsed.netloc, parsed.path.lstrip("/")
+
+
+def _build_asset_metadata(source: KnowledgeSource) -> DocumentV1AssetMetadata:
+    return DocumentV1AssetMetadata(
+        business_id=source.business_id,
+        asset_id=source.id,
+        mime_type=source.mime_type or "",
+        file_name=source.file_name or "",
+        s3_uri=source.s3_uri,
+    )
+
+
+async def _fetch_s3_bytes(s3_client: S3GetObjectClient, bucket: str, key: str) -> BytesIO:
+    """Wrap S3 fetch errors as TransientIndexerError — Lambda async retry will
+    re-attempt. A persistently-missing object will eventually hit DLQ; the
+    operator can mark the row failed by hand."""
+    try:
+        response = await s3_client.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)
+        body = response["Body"]
+        raw = await body.read()
+    except Exception as e:
+        raise TransientIndexerError(f"s3 fetch failed: {e}") from e
+    return BytesIO(raw)
+
+
+async def _parse_document(
+    parser: DocumentParser,
+    raw: BytesIO,
+    metadata: DocumentV1AssetMetadata,
+) -> DocumentV1:
+    """Wrap parser errors as PermanentIndexerError — re-parsing identical bytes
+    will fail identically. PermanentIndexerError raised from inside the parser
+    is preserved verbatim."""
+    try:
+        return await parser.async_parse_document(raw, metadata)
+    except PermanentIndexerError:
+        raise
+    except Exception as e:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError(f"parse failed: {e}") from e
+
+
+async def _ingest_with_clean_slate(
+    knowledge_base: IndexableKnowledgeBase,
+    document: DocumentV1,
+) -> None:
+    """Pre-emptive delete makes reindex idempotent (§5.3 step 7) AND ensures
+    that a partial-write failure on a re-attempt won't accumulate orphan
+    vectors from earlier ingests. Both calls wrapped as transient — they hit
+    Qdrant + Dynamo where most errors are network/throttle blips."""
+    try:
+        await knowledge_base.async_delete(document.id, document.business_id)
+    except Exception as e:
+        raise TransientIndexerError(f"pre-ingest delete failed: {e}") from e
+    try:
+        await knowledge_base.async_ingest(document)
+    except Exception as e:
+        raise TransientIndexerError(f"ingest failed: {e}") from e
+
+
+async def _mark_failed(
+    source_repo: SourceRepository,
+    source_id: ULID,
+    error: Exception,
+) -> None:
+    message = str(error)[:_ERROR_MESSAGE_LIMIT] or error.__class__.__name__
+    await source_repo.async_transition(
+        source_id,
+        expected=KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXING,
+        new=KnowledgeSourceStatus.FAILED,
+        error_message=message,
+    )
+
+
+async def async_index_source(
+    source_id: ULID,
+    business_id: ULID,
+    *,
+    source_repo: SourceRepository,
+    knowledge_base: IndexableKnowledgeBase,
+    parser: DocumentParser,
+    s3_client: S3GetObjectClient,
+) -> None:
+    """Drive `source_id` from pending_upload / indexed / failed → indexed.
+
+    Returns cleanly when:
+      - source is missing or soft-deleted,
+      - source is already in a non-reindexable state (`indexing`, `deleted`),
+      - CAS to `indexing` loses to a racing caller,
+      - a PermanentIndexerError is raised (row is marked `failed`).
+
+    Re-raises when a TransientIndexerError or unexpected exception occurs so
+    Lambda async retry / DLQ can take over (row is still marked `failed`).
+    """
+    with bound_contextvars(
+        source_id=str(source_id),
+        business_id=str(business_id),
+    ):
+        source = await source_repo.async_get_by_id(source_id, business_id)
+        if source is None:
+            logger.info("indexer: source missing or soft-deleted; skipping")
+            return
+        if source.status not in _REINDEXABLE_STATUSES:
+            logger.info(
+                "indexer: source not in a reindexable state; skipping",
+                status=source.status.value,
+            )
+            return
+
+        # CAS to `indexing`. A None return means a concurrent caller won.
+        txn = await source_repo.async_transition(
+            source_id,
+            expected=source.status,
+            new=KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXING,
+        )
+        if txn is None:
+            logger.info(
+                "indexer: lost CAS to another caller; skipping",
+                expected_status=source.status.value,
+            )
+            return
+
+        try:
+            _validate_pre_parse(txn)
+            assert txn.s3_uri is not None  # validated above
+            bucket, key = _parse_s3_uri(txn.s3_uri)
+            raw = await _fetch_s3_bytes(s3_client, bucket, key)
+            metadata = _build_asset_metadata(txn)
+            document = await _parse_document(parser, raw, metadata)
+            await _ingest_with_clean_slate(knowledge_base, document)
+        except PermanentIndexerError as e:
+            logger.exception("indexer: permanent failure; marking row failed")
+            await _mark_failed(source_repo, source_id, e)
+            return
+        except Exception as e:
+            logger.exception("indexer: transient failure; marking row failed and re-raising")
+            await _mark_failed(source_repo, source_id, e)
+            raise
+
+        await source_repo.async_transition(
+            source_id,
+            expected=KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXING,
+            new=KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXED,
+            indexed_at=datetime.utcnow(),
+        )
+        logger.info("indexer: source indexed")
+
+
+def _validate_pre_parse(source: KnowledgeSource) -> None:
+    """Enforce the §9.2 guards before we burn cycles on parsing."""
+    if source.mime_type is None:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError("source row missing mime_type")
+    if source.file_size_bytes is None:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError("source row missing file_size_bytes")
+    if source.s3_uri is None:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError("source row missing s3_uri")
+    validate_mime_type(source.mime_type)
+    validate_file_size(source.file_size_bytes)
+
+
+__all__ = [
+    "DocumentParser",
+    "IndexableKnowledgeBase",
+    "S3GetObjectClient",
+    "SourceRepository",
+    "async_index_source",
+]

@@ -0,0 +1,229 @@
+"""FAQ 2.0 unstructured-knowledge models — pure data + validation, no I/O.
+
+Backing plan: FAQ_V2_INFRA_PLAN.md §4.1 (registry schema), §5.2 (search result
+shapes), §6.3 (error taxonomy), §9.2 (guardrails).
+"""
+
+from datetime import datetime
+from typing import Any, Sequence
+
+from pydantic import Field
+from tenets.identifier import ULID
+from tenets.utility_classes import ConnectlyBaseModel, StrEnum
+
+
+# Per FAQ_V2_INFRA_PLAN.md §3.4 — `source_type` keyword stamped on every
+# Qdrant payload and `knowledge_sources` row.
+class SourceType(StrEnum):
+    PDF = "pdf"
+    DOCX = "docx"
+    TXT = "txt"
+    CSV = "csv"
+    XLSX = "xlsx"
+    MANUAL_TEXT = "manual_text"
+    FAQ_PAIR = "faq_pair"
+
+
+class KnowledgeSourceStatus(StrEnum):
+    PENDING_UPLOAD = "pending_upload"
+    INDEXING = "indexing"
+    INDEXED = "indexed"
+    FAILED = "failed"
+    DELETED = "deleted"
+
+
+# Per §4.1 state machine. Transitions to DELETED are allowed from any
+# non-deleted state (soft-delete via DeleteKnowledgeSource RPC).
+# INDEXING → PENDING_UPLOAD is the reconciliation-DAG reset for rows stuck in
+# INDEXING past the lambda timeout (§9.3).
+VALID_TRANSITIONS: dict[KnowledgeSourceStatus, frozenset[KnowledgeSourceStatus]] = {
+    KnowledgeSourceStatus.PENDING_UPLOAD: frozenset(
+        {
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXING,
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.DELETED,
+        }
+    ),
+    KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXING: frozenset(
+        {
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXED,
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.FAILED,
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.PENDING_UPLOAD,
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.DELETED,
+        }
+    ),
+    KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXED: frozenset(
+        {
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXING,
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.DELETED,
+        }
+    ),
+    KnowledgeSourceStatus.FAILED: frozenset(
+        {
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXING,
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.DELETED,
+        }
+    ),
+    KnowledgeSourceStatus.DELETED: frozenset(),
+}
+
+
+# §9.2 — MIME allowlist. Source of truth for both the API pre-upload check
+# and the lambda's defense-in-depth post-parse check.
+MIME_TYPE_ALLOWLIST: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
+    {
+        "application/pdf",
+        "text/plain",
+        "text/csv",
+        "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet",
+        "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document",
+    }
+)
+
+# §9.2 — default 50 MB cap (also enforced by presigned-URL Content-Length).
+MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES: int = 50 * 1024 * 1024
+
+# Canonical mapping between file-bound `SourceType`s and their MIME / extension.
+# `manual_text` and `faq_pair` are not file-uploadable in this phase, so they're
+# absent from both maps.
+SOURCE_TYPE_TO_MIME: dict["SourceType", str] = {}  # populated below
+SOURCE_TYPE_TO_EXTENSION: dict["SourceType", str] = {}  # populated below
+
+
+class TransientIndexerError(Exception):
+    """Re-raised by the lambda so async retry / DLQ kicks in. Use for
+    OpenAI / Qdrant / network blips that should be retried (§6.3)."""
+
+
+class PermanentIndexerError(Exception):
+    """Swallowed by the lambda after marking the row `failed`. Use for
+    parse errors, oversize files, unsupported MIME — anything that won't
+    succeed on retry (§6.3)."""
+
+
+class KnowledgeSource(ConnectlyBaseModel):
+    """Mirror of the `knowledge_sources` Postgres row (§4.1)."""
+
+    id: ULID = Field(default_factory=ULID.new)
+    business_id: ULID
+    source_type: SourceType
+    title: str | None = None
+    file_name: str | None = None
+    mime_type: str | None = None
+    s3_uri: str | None = None
+    checksum: str | None = None
+    file_size_bytes: int | None = None
+    status: KnowledgeSourceStatus
+    version: int = 1
+    metadata: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
+    error_message: str | None = None
+    indexed_at: datetime | None = None
+    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
+    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
+    deleted_at: datetime | None = None
+
+
+class KnowledgeRetrievalResult(ConnectlyBaseModel):
+    """One ranked chunk returned by `qa_engine_v2.async_search` (§5.2)."""
+
+    source_id: ULID
+    source_type: SourceType
+    source_title: str | None = None
+    chunk_id: ULID
+    text: str
+    score: float
+    normalized_score: float
+    metadata: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
+
+
+class SearchSummary(ConnectlyBaseModel):
+    """Aggregated confidence signals for a single search call (§5.2)."""
+
+    result_count: int
+    top_score: float | None = None
+    top_score_gap: float | None = None
+    has_result_above_threshold: bool = False
+
+
+class SearchKnowledgeResult(ConnectlyBaseModel):
+    results: list[KnowledgeRetrievalResult]
+    summary: SearchSummary
+
+
+class RawChunkHit(ConnectlyBaseModel):
+    """Raw chunk + score returned by the knowledge base before normalization.
+    The engine fills in `normalized_score` and wraps these in
+    `KnowledgeRetrievalResult`s."""
+
+    chunk_id: ULID
+    source_id: ULID
+    source_type: SourceType
+    source_title: str | None = None
+    text: str
+    score: float
+    metadata: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
+
+
+def is_valid_transition(
+    from_status: KnowledgeSourceStatus,
+    to_status: KnowledgeSourceStatus,
+) -> bool:
+    return to_status in VALID_TRANSITIONS.get(from_status, frozenset())
+
+
+def validate_mime_type(mime_type: str) -> None:
+    """Raise PermanentIndexerError if `mime_type` isn't on the allowlist."""
+    if mime_type not in MIME_TYPE_ALLOWLIST:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError(
+            f"unsupported mime_type {mime_type!r}; " f"allowed: {sorted(MIME_TYPE_ALLOWLIST)}"
+        )
+
+
+def validate_file_size(
+    file_size_bytes: int,
+    *,
+    max_bytes: int = MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES,
+) -> None:
+    """Raise PermanentIndexerError if file exceeds `max_bytes` or is non-positive."""
+    if file_size_bytes <= 0:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError(f"file_size_bytes must be positive, got {file_size_bytes}")
+    if file_size_bytes > max_bytes:
+        raise PermanentIndexerError(f"file_size_bytes {file_size_bytes} exceeds cap {max_bytes}")
+
+
+SOURCE_TYPE_TO_MIME.update(
+    {
+        SourceType.PDF: "application/pdf",
+        SourceType.DOCX: (
+            "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document"
+        ),
+        SourceType.TXT: "text/plain",
+        SourceType.CSV: "text/csv",
+        SourceType.XLSX: ("application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet"),
+    }
+)
+SOURCE_TYPE_TO_EXTENSION.update(
+    {
+        SourceType.PDF: "pdf",
+        SourceType.DOCX: "docx",
+        SourceType.TXT: "txt",
+        SourceType.CSV: "csv",
+        SourceType.XLSX: "xlsx",
+    }
+)
+
+
+def compute_normalized_scores(scores: Sequence[float]) -> list[float]:
+    """Divide every score by the max score so the top result is 1.0.
+
+    Edge cases:
+      - empty input → empty list
+      - max score ≤ 0 (e.g., all zeros, all negative) → list of zeros, since
+        a meaningful normalization is undefined and downstream callers should
+        treat these as no-confidence rather than raise.
+    """
+    if not scores:
+        return []
+    top = max(scores)
+    if top <= 0:
+        return [0.0 for _ in scores]
+    return [s / top for s in scores]

@@ -0,0 +1,497 @@
+"""FAQ 2.0 engine — public API surface called by the RPC layer and the lambda.
+
+Each lifecycle method orchestrates a small set of injected primitives:
+    repo (Postgres) → S3 client → knowledge base → indexer
+
+There is no auth check here; callers (RPC handlers) are responsible for
+verifying `auth_context.business_id == request.business_id` before reaching
+this engine. Tenant isolation in this layer is purely a function of every
+method requiring `business_id` and the repo enforcing it on every query
+(FAQ_V2_INFRA_PLAN.md §7.3).
+
+Backing plan: §5.2 (lifecycle), §5.4 (search), §7.2 (endpoint behavior).
+"""
+
+from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
+from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
+from typing import Any, AsyncIterator, Awaitable, Callable, Protocol, Sequence, cast
+
+import structlog
+from aiobotocore.session import AioSession, get_session
+from asyncpg import UniqueViolationError
+from ocean_beach.knowledge_bases.knowledge_base_v2 import KnowledgeBaseV2
+from ocean_beach.knowledge_bases.knowledge_base_v2.document_parser_v1 import (
+    DocumentParserV1,
+)
+from ocean_beach.models.ecommerce.resources_v1 import KnowledgeBaseResourceV1
+from pydantic import Field
+from structlog.contextvars import bound_contextvars
+from tenets.identifier import ULID, AppEnv
+from tenets.utility_classes import ConnectlyBaseModel
+
+from . import indexer as _indexer
+from .indexer import IndexableKnowledgeBase
+from .models import (
+    MIME_TYPE_ALLOWLIST,
+    SOURCE_TYPE_TO_EXTENSION,
+    SOURCE_TYPE_TO_MIME,
+    KnowledgeRetrievalResult,
+    KnowledgeSource,
+    KnowledgeSourceStatus,
+    RawChunkHit,
+    SearchKnowledgeResult,
+    SearchSummary,
+    SourceType,
+    compute_normalized_scores,
+    validate_mime_type,
+)
+from .source_repository import KnowledgeSourceRepository
+
+logger: structlog.stdlib.BoundLogger = structlog.get_logger()
+
+DEFAULT_MAX_DAILY_SOURCES_PER_BUSINESS = 100
+DEFAULT_PRESIGNED_URL_TTL_SECONDS = 15 * 60
+DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS = 10
+DEFAULT_USE_SPARSE = False
+
+# Source types this phase can ingest from S3. `manual_text` and `faq_pair` are
+# explicitly excluded — they're file-less and addressed in later phases.
+_FILE_BOUND_SOURCE_TYPES: frozenset[SourceType] = frozenset(SOURCE_TYPE_TO_MIME.keys())
+
+
+# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Domain exceptions — RPC layer maps these to gRPC status codes.
+# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+class SourceNotFound(Exception):
+    """Source missing or owned by a different tenant. Maps to NOT_FOUND."""
+
+
+class DuplicateSource(Exception):
+    """A non-deleted source with the same checksum already exists for this
+    tenant. Maps to ALREADY_EXISTS."""
+
+
+class QuotaExceeded(Exception):
+    """Business has hit its daily upload quota. Maps to RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED."""
+
+
+class InvalidSourceState(Exception):
+    """The source is in a state that doesn't allow this operation (e.g.,
+    reindex on `pending_upload`). Maps to FAILED_PRECONDITION."""
+
+
+class InvalidSourceArgument(Exception):
+    """Caller supplied an invalid mime/source_type/etc. Maps to INVALID_ARGUMENT."""
+
+
+# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Protocols — the engine never imports concrete S3/KB classes for typing,
+# only structural shapes. Lets tests pass thin AsyncMocks.
+# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+class S3Client(Protocol):
+    def generate_presigned_url(
+        self, ClientMethod: str, Params: dict[str, Any], ExpiresIn: int
+    ) -> str: ...
+
+    async def copy_object(self, **kwargs: Any) -> dict[str, Any]: ...
+
+    async def get_object(self, *, Bucket: str, Key: str) -> dict[str, Any]: ...
+
+
+class SearchableKnowledgeBase(IndexableKnowledgeBase, Protocol):
+    async def async_query_chunks(
+        self,
+        business_id: ULID,
+        query: str,
+        *,
+        max_results: int,
+        use_sparse: bool,
+        source_types: Sequence[SourceType],
+        source_ids: Sequence[ULID] | None,
+        include_neighbor_chunks: bool,
+    ) -> list[RawChunkHit]: ...
+
+
+# Optional resource lookup. If callable returns None the engine falls back to
+# hard-coded defaults.
+KBResourceProvider = Callable[[ULID], Awaitable[KnowledgeBaseResourceV1 | None]]
+
+
+# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Result types specific to engine API.
+# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+class PresignedUploadURL(ConnectlyBaseModel):
+    url: str
+    method: str = "PUT"
+    headers: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict)
+    expires_in_seconds: int = DEFAULT_PRESIGNED_URL_TTL_SECONDS
+
+
+# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Engine.
+# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+class QuestionAnswerEngineV2:
+    def __init__(
+        self,
+        env: AppEnv,
+        s3_bucket: str,
+        *,
+        source_repository: KnowledgeSourceRepository | None = None,
+        knowledge_base: SearchableKnowledgeBase | None = None,
+        document_parser: DocumentParserV1 | None = None,
+        session: AioSession | None = None,
+        s3_client: S3Client | None = None,
+        kb_resource_provider: KBResourceProvider | None = None,
+        max_daily_sources: int = DEFAULT_MAX_DAILY_SOURCES_PER_BUSINESS,
+        presigned_url_ttl_seconds: int = DEFAULT_PRESIGNED_URL_TTL_SECONDS,
+    ):
+        self.env = env
+        self.s3_bucket = s3_bucket
+        self.source_repository = source_repository or KnowledgeSourceRepository(env)
+        # Concrete KnowledgeBaseV2 satisfies SearchableKnowledgeBase structurally
+        # once the search method is added; until then prod callers pass a real
+        # KB and tests pass a mock that conforms.
+        self.knowledge_base: SearchableKnowledgeBase = knowledge_base  # type: ignore[assignment]
+        if self.knowledge_base is None:
+            self.knowledge_base = KnowledgeBaseV2(env)  # type: ignore[assignment]
+        self.document_parser = document_parser or DocumentParserV1()
+        # Two ways to wire S3:
+        #   - `session`: per-call client (matches the codebase convention used
+        #     by KnowledgeBaseV2, DocumentParserV1, file_manager, etc.)
+        #   - `s3_client`: a pre-opened client used directly. Used by the
+        #     lambda (one outer `async with` for the whole event loop) and by
+        #     unit tests (AsyncMock). Takes precedence when set.
+        self.session = session or get_session()
+        self.s3_client = s3_client
+        self.kb_resource_provider = kb_resource_provider
+        self.max_daily_sources = max_daily_sources
+        self.presigned_url_ttl_seconds = presigned_url_ttl_seconds
+
+    @asynccontextmanager
+    async def _s3_client(self) -> AsyncIterator[S3Client]:
+        """Yield an S3 client. If a long-lived `s3_client` was injected, yield
+        it as-is (no enter/exit). Otherwise open a fresh per-call client from
+        `self.session`."""
+        if self.s3_client is not None:
+            yield self.s3_client
+            return
+        async with self.session.create_client("s3") as client:
+            yield cast(S3Client, client)
+
+    # ---- helpers ------------------------------------------------------------
+
+    def _s3_key(self, business_id: ULID, source_id: ULID, source_type: SourceType) -> str:
+        ext = SOURCE_TYPE_TO_EXTENSION.get(source_type)
+        if ext is None:
+            raise InvalidSourceArgument(f"source_type {source_type.value} is not file-uploadable")
+        return f"faq_v2/{business_id}/{source_id}.{ext}"
+
+    async def _enforce_quota(self, business_id: ULID) -> None:
+        since = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=24)
+        count = await self.source_repository.async_count_since(business_id, since)
+        if count >= self.max_daily_sources:
+            raise QuotaExceeded(
+                f"business {business_id} hit daily quota " f"({count}/{self.max_daily_sources})"
+            )
+
+    async def _require_source(self, source_id: ULID, business_id: ULID) -> KnowledgeSource:
+        source = await self.source_repository.async_get_by_id(source_id, business_id)
+        if source is None:
+            raise SourceNotFound(f"source {source_id} not found for business {business_id}")
+        return source
+
+    # ---- lifecycle ----------------------------------------------------------
+
+    async def async_create_source(
+        self,
+        *,
+        business_id: ULID,
+        source_type: SourceType,
+        mime_type: str,
+        file_name: str,
+        file_size_bytes: int,
+        title: str | None = None,
+        checksum: str | None = None,
+        metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
+    ) -> tuple[KnowledgeSource, PresignedUploadURL]:
+        """Create a `pending_upload` row and return a presigned PUT URL.
+
+        Validates source_type/mime compatibility, MIME allowlist, daily
+        quota. Lets `asyncpg.UniqueViolationError` from the partial unique
+        index on (business_id, checksum) bubble up as `DuplicateSource`.
+        """
+        if source_type not in _FILE_BOUND_SOURCE_TYPES:
+            raise InvalidSourceArgument(
+                f"source_type {source_type.value} is not file-uploadable in this phase"
+            )
+        if mime_type not in MIME_TYPE_ALLOWLIST:
+            raise InvalidSourceArgument(f"mime_type {mime_type!r} is not on the allowlist")
+        if SOURCE_TYPE_TO_MIME[source_type] != mime_type:
+            raise InvalidSourceArgument(
+                f"mime_type {mime_type!r} doesn't match source_type {source_type.value}"
+            )
+
+        validate_mime_type(mime_type)
+        await self._enforce_quota(business_id)
+
+        # Pre-compute the source_id so we can stamp the canonical s3_uri on
+        # the row at insert time. (Lambda still HEAD's the object post-upload
+        # to populate `checksum` + `file_size_bytes` from the actual bytes.)
+        source_id = ULID.new()
+        key = self._s3_key(business_id, source_id, source_type)
+        s3_uri = f"s3://{self.s3_bucket}/{key}"
+
+        try:
+            source = await self.source_repository.async_create(
+                id=source_id,
+                business_id=business_id,
+                source_type=source_type,
+                title=title,
+                file_name=file_name,
+                mime_type=mime_type,
+                s3_uri=s3_uri,
+                checksum=checksum,
+                file_size_bytes=file_size_bytes,
+                metadata=metadata,
+            )
+        except UniqueViolationError as e:
+            raise DuplicateSource(
+                f"source with checksum {checksum} already exists for business {business_id}"
+            ) from e
+
+        async with self._s3_client() as s3:
+            url = s3.generate_presigned_url(
+                ClientMethod="put_object",
+                Params={
+                    "Bucket": self.s3_bucket,
+                    "Key": key,
+                    "ContentType": mime_type,
+                },
+                ExpiresIn=self.presigned_url_ttl_seconds,
+            )
+        return source, PresignedUploadURL(
+            url=url,
+            method="PUT",
+            headers={"Content-Type": mime_type},
+            expires_in_seconds=self.presigned_url_ttl_seconds,
+        )
+
+    async def async_get_source(self, source_id: ULID, business_id: ULID) -> KnowledgeSource:
+        return await self._require_source(source_id, business_id)
+
+    async def async_list_sources(
+        self,
+        business_id: ULID,
+        *,
+        statuses: Sequence[KnowledgeSourceStatus] | None = None,
+        source_types: Sequence[SourceType] | None = None,
+        limit: int = 100,
+        offset: int = 0,
+    ) -> Sequence[KnowledgeSource]:
+        return await self.source_repository.async_list(
+            business_id,
+            statuses=statuses,
+            source_types=source_types,
+            limit=limit,
+            offset=offset,
+        )
+
+    async def async_delete_source(self, source_id: ULID, business_id: ULID) -> None:
+        """Soft-delete the source row + drop chunks/vectors. S3 raw object
+        is retained per §7.2."""
+        source = await self._require_source(source_id, business_id)
+        if source.status == KnowledgeSourceStatus.DELETED:
+            return  # idempotent
+        await self.knowledge_base.async_delete(source_id, business_id)
+        await self.source_repository.async_soft_delete(source_id, business_id)
+
+    async def async_reindex_source(self, source_id: ULID, business_id: ULID) -> None:
+        """Trigger a reindex via S3 self-copy (§3.5). New eTag → fresh Redis
+        lock → lambda re-fires."""
+        source = await self._require_source(source_id, business_id)
+        if source.status not in (
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.INDEXED,
+            KnowledgeSourceStatus.FAILED,
+        ):
+            raise InvalidSourceState(
+                f"reindex requires status in (indexed, failed); current: {source.status.value}"
+            )
+        if source.s3_uri is None:
+            raise InvalidSourceState(f"source {source_id} has no s3_uri; cannot self-copy")
+
+        bucket, key = _parse_s3_uri(source.s3_uri)
+        # MetadataDirective='REPLACE' forces a new eTag, which the lambda's
+        # eTag-keyed Redis lock treats as a fresh event (§6.2 step 5).
+        async with self._s3_client() as s3:
+            await s3.copy_object(
+                Bucket=bucket,
+                Key=key,
+                CopySource={"Bucket": bucket, "Key": key},
+                MetadataDirective="REPLACE",
+                ContentType=source.mime_type or "application/octet-stream",
+            )
+
+    async def async_index_source(self, source_id: ULID, business_id: ULID) -> None:
+        """Engine-level handle to the indexer module — what the lambda calls.
+        Delegates to the standalone `async_index_source` with engine-bound
+        deps."""
+        async with self._s3_client() as s3:
+            await _indexer.async_index_source(
+                source_id,
+                business_id,
+                source_repo=self.source_repository,
+                knowledge_base=self.knowledge_base,
+                parser=self.document_parser,
+                s3_client=s3,
+            )
+
+    # ---- search -------------------------------------------------------------
+
+    async def async_search(
+        self,
+        business_id: ULID,
+        query: str,
+        *,
+        max_results: int | None = None,
+        source_types: Sequence[SourceType] | None = None,
+        source_ids: Sequence[ULID] | None = None,
+        min_score: float | None = None,
+        use_sparse: bool | None = None,
+        include_neighbor_chunks: bool = False,
+    ) -> SearchKnowledgeResult:
+        """Wrap the KB query + apply normalization + summary.
+
+        Filter construction always includes `source_type IN (...)` — never
+        rely on payload defaults, since legacy v3 chunks have
+        `source_type=null` and would otherwise leak into FAQ 2.0 results
+        (§3.4 mitigation).
+        """
+        with bound_contextvars(business_id=str(business_id)):
+            resolved_max_results, resolved_use_sparse = await self._resolve_search_defaults(
+                business_id,
+                explicit_max_results=max_results,
+                explicit_use_sparse=use_sparse,
+            )
+            resolved_source_types = (
+                tuple(source_types) if source_types is not None else tuple(_FILE_BOUND_SOURCE_TYPES)
+            )
+
+            raw_hits = await self.knowledge_base.async_query_chunks(
+                business_id,
+                query,
+                max_results=resolved_max_results,
+                use_sparse=resolved_use_sparse,
+                source_types=resolved_source_types,
+                source_ids=source_ids,
+                include_neighbor_chunks=include_neighbor_chunks,
+            )
+
+            if min_score is not None:
+                raw_hits = [h for h in raw_hits if h.score >= min_score]
+
+            normalized = compute_normalized_scores([h.score for h in raw_hits])
+            results = [
+                KnowledgeRetrievalResult(
+                    source_id=hit.source_id,
+                    source_type=hit.source_type,
+                    source_title=hit.source_title,
+                    chunk_id=hit.chunk_id,
+                    text=hit.text,
+                    score=hit.score,
+                    normalized_score=normalized[i],
+                    metadata=hit.metadata,
+                )
+                for i, hit in enumerate(raw_hits)
+            ]
+            summary = _build_summary(results, min_score=min_score)
+            return SearchKnowledgeResult(results=results, summary=summary)
+
+    async def _resolve_search_defaults(
+        self,
+        business_id: ULID,
+        *,
+        explicit_max_results: int | None,
+        explicit_use_sparse: bool | None,
+    ) -> tuple[int, bool]:
+        """Caller-supplied args win; otherwise read from KnowledgeBaseResourceV1
+        if a provider was wired; otherwise fall back to module defaults."""
+        if explicit_max_results is not None and explicit_use_sparse is not None:
+            return explicit_max_results, explicit_use_sparse
+
+        resource: KnowledgeBaseResourceV1 | None = None
+        if self.kb_resource_provider is not None:
+            resource = await self.kb_resource_provider(business_id)
+
+        max_results = (
+            explicit_max_results
+            if explicit_max_results is not None
+            else (
+                resource.max_results if resource and resource.max_results else DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS
+            )
+        )
+        use_sparse = (
+            explicit_use_sparse
+            if explicit_use_sparse is not None
+            else (resource.use_sparse_vectors if resource else DEFAULT_USE_SPARSE)
+        )
+        return max_results, use_sparse
+
+
+def _build_summary(
+    results: Sequence[KnowledgeRetrievalResult],
+    *,
+    min_score: float | None,
+) -> SearchSummary:
+    if not results:
+        return SearchSummary(
+            result_count=0,
+            top_score=None,
+            top_score_gap=None,
+            has_result_above_threshold=False,
+        )
+    top = results[0].score
+    second = results[1].score if len(results) > 1 else 0.0
+    gap = top - second
+    above_threshold = (min_score is None) or (top >= min_score)
+    return SearchSummary(
+        result_count=len(results),
+        top_score=top,
+        top_score_gap=gap,
+        has_result_above_threshold=above_threshold,
+    )
+
+
+def _parse_s3_uri(s3_uri: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
+    if not s3_uri.startswith("s3://"):
+        raise InvalidSourceArgument(f"malformed s3_uri: {s3_uri!r}")
+    rest = s3_uri[len("s3://") :]
+    bucket, _, key = rest.partition("/")
+    if not bucket or not key:
+        raise InvalidSourceArgument(f"malformed s3_uri: {s3_uri!r}")
+    return bucket, key
+
+
+__all__ = [
+    "DEFAULT_MAX_DAILY_SOURCES_PER_BUSINESS",
+    "DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS",
+    "DEFAULT_PRESIGNED_URL_TTL_SECONDS",
+    "DEFAULT_USE_SPARSE",
+    "DuplicateSource",
+    "InvalidSourceArgument",
+    "InvalidSourceState",
+    "KBResourceProvider",
+    "PresignedUploadURL",
+    "QuestionAnswerEngineV2",
+    "QuotaExceeded",
+    "S3Client",
+    "SearchableKnowledgeBase",
+    "SourceNotFound",
+]

@@ -0,0 +1,320 @@
+"""Postgres repository for the FAQ 2.0 `knowledge_sources` registry.
+
+Sole owner of `knowledge_sources` SQL. Other layers (RPC handlers, indexer
+lambda) call into this module — they never write the table directly.
+
+Backing plan: FAQ_V2_INFRA_PLAN.md §4.1 (schema), §5.3 (transition usage).
+"""
+
+import json
+from datetime import datetime
+from typing import Any, Sequence
+
+import structlog
+from asyncpg import Record
+from tenets.db import PostgresDB
+from tenets.identifier import ULID, AppEnv
+
+from .models import (
+    VALID_TRANSITIONS,
+    KnowledgeSource,
+    KnowledgeSourceStatus,
+    SourceType,
+    is_valid_transition,
+)
+
+logger: structlog.stdlib.BoundLogger = structlog.get_logger()
+
+
+_ALL_COLUMNS = (
+    "id, business_id, source_type, title, file_name, mime_type, "
+    "s3_uri, checksum, file_size_bytes, status, version, metadata, "
+    "error_message, indexed_at, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at"
+)
+
+# Columns the lambda / indexer is allowed to set during a status transition.
+# Keep this tight — it's used to whitelist dynamic column names interpolated
+# into the UPDATE's SET clause.
+_MUTABLE_COLUMNS: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
+    {
+        "title",
+        "file_name",
+        "mime_type",
+        "s3_uri",
+        "checksum",
+        "file_size_bytes",
+        "version",
+        "metadata",
+        "error_message",
+        "indexed_at",
+    }
+)
+
+
+def _row_to_source(row: Record) -> KnowledgeSource:
+    raw_metadata: Any = row["metadata"]
+    if isinstance(raw_metadata, str):
+        # asyncpg returns jsonb as str unless a codec is registered (it isn't,
+        # in either prod init_conn or the testcontainer pool).
+        metadata: dict[str, Any] = json.loads(raw_metadata) if raw_metadata else {}
+    elif raw_metadata is None:
+        metadata = {}
+    else:
+        metadata = raw_metadata
+    return KnowledgeSource(
+        id=row["id"],
+        business_id=row["business_id"],
+        source_type=row["source_type"],
+        title=row["title"],
+        file_name=row["file_name"],
+        mime_type=row["mime_type"],
+        s3_uri=row["s3_uri"],
+        checksum=row["checksum"],
+        file_size_bytes=row["file_size_bytes"],
+        status=row["status"],
+        version=row["version"],
+        metadata=metadata,
+        error_message=row["error_message"],
+        indexed_at=row["indexed_at"],
+        created_at=row["created_at"],
+        updated_at=row["updated_at"],
+        deleted_at=row["deleted_at"],
+    )
+
+
+class IllegalTransitionError(ValueError):
+    """Raised when a caller asks for a (from → to) pair not in VALID_TRANSITIONS.
+    This is a programmer error, not a race — races return None instead."""
+
+
+class KnowledgeSourceRepository:
+    def __init__(self, env: AppEnv, db: PostgresDB | None = None) -> None:
+        self.env = env
+        self.db = db if db is not None else PostgresDB(env)
+
+    async def async_create(
+        self,
+        *,
+        business_id: ULID,
+        source_type: SourceType,
+        status: KnowledgeSourceStatus = KnowledgeSourceStatus.PENDING_UPLOAD,
+        id: ULID | None = None,
+        title: str | None = None,
+        file_name: str | None = None,
+        mime_type: str | None = None,
+        s3_uri: str | None = None,
+        checksum: str | None = None,
+        file_size_bytes: int | None = None,
+        metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
+    ) -> KnowledgeSource:
+        """Insert a new knowledge_sources row.
+
+        Raises asyncpg.UniqueViolationError if (business_id, checksum) collides
+        with an existing non-deleted row — let the caller decide how to surface
+        that (e.g., RPC layer maps to ALREADY_EXISTS).
+        """
+        source_id = id if id is not None else ULID.new()
+        metadata_json = json.dumps(metadata or {})
+        sql = f"""
+            INSERT INTO knowledge_sources (
+                id, business_id, source_type, title, file_name, mime_type,
+                s3_uri, checksum, file_size_bytes, status, metadata
+            )
+            VALUES (
+                $1::uuid, $2::uuid, $3, $4, $5, $6,
+                $7, $8, $9, $10, $11::jsonb
+            )
+            RETURNING {_ALL_COLUMNS}
+        """
+        rows = await self.db.query(
+            sql,
+            (
+                str(source_id),
+                str(business_id),
+                str(source_type),
+                title,
+                file_name,
+                mime_type,
+                s3_uri,
+                checksum,
+                file_size_bytes,
+                str(status),
+                metadata_json,
+            ),
+        )
+        return _row_to_source(rows[0])
+
+    async def async_get_by_id(
+        self,
+        source_id: ULID,
+        business_id: ULID,
+        *,
+        include_deleted: bool = False,
+    ) -> KnowledgeSource | None:
+        sql = f"""
+            SELECT {_ALL_COLUMNS}
+              FROM knowledge_sources
+             WHERE id = $1::uuid
+               AND business_id = $2::uuid
+        """
+        if not include_deleted:
+            sql += " AND deleted_at IS NULL"
+        rows = await self.db.query(sql, (str(source_id), str(business_id)))
+        if not rows:
+            return None
+        return _row_to_source(rows[0])
+
+    async def async_list(
+        self,
+        business_id: ULID,
+        *,
+        statuses: Sequence[KnowledgeSourceStatus] | None = None,
+        source_types: Sequence[SourceType] | None = None,
+        include_deleted: bool = False,
+        limit: int = 100,
+        offset: int = 0,
+    ) -> Sequence[KnowledgeSource]:
+        where: list[str] = ["business_id = $1::uuid"]
+        params: list[Any] = [str(business_id)]
+
+        if not include_deleted:
+            where.append("deleted_at IS NULL")
+
+        if statuses:
+            params.append([str(s) for s in statuses])
+            where.append(f"status = ANY(${len(params)}::varchar[])")
+
+        if source_types:
+            params.append([str(t) for t in source_types])
+            where.append(f"source_type = ANY(${len(params)}::varchar[])")
+
+        params.append(limit)
+        limit_idx = len(params)
+        params.append(offset)
+        offset_idx = len(params)
+
+        sql = f"""
+            SELECT {_ALL_COLUMNS}
+              FROM knowledge_sources
+             WHERE {' AND '.join(where)}
+             ORDER BY created_at DESC
+             LIMIT ${limit_idx}
+            OFFSET ${offset_idx}
+        """
+        rows = await self.db.query(sql, params)
+        return [_row_to_source(r) for r in rows]
+
+    async def async_transition(
+        self,
+        source_id: ULID,
+        *,
+        expected: KnowledgeSourceStatus,
+        new: KnowledgeSourceStatus,
+        **field_updates: Any,
+    ) -> KnowledgeSource | None:
+        """Atomic compare-and-swap on `status`.
+
+        Returns the updated row on success, None if the precondition didn't
+        hold (race lost, row missing, soft-deleted, or status moved on).
+        Raises IllegalTransitionError if the (expected → new) pair isn't in
+        VALID_TRANSITIONS — that's a programmer bug, not a race.
+
+        `field_updates` may set any column in `_MUTABLE_COLUMNS`. Unknown
+        columns raise ValueError; this is the SQL-injection guard since the
+        keys land in the SET clause.
+        """
+        if not is_valid_transition(expected, new):
+            allowed = sorted(s.value for s in VALID_TRANSITIONS.get(expected, frozenset()))
+            raise IllegalTransitionError(
+                f"transition {expected.value} → {new.value} is not allowed; "
+                f"valid next states from {expected.value}: {allowed}"
+            )
+
+        unknown = set(field_updates) - _MUTABLE_COLUMNS
+        if unknown:
+            raise ValueError(
+                f"field_updates contains non-mutable columns: {sorted(unknown)}; "
+                f"allowed: {sorted(_MUTABLE_COLUMNS)}"
+            )
+
+        # Param order: $1 new_status, $2 source_id, $3 expected_status, then
+        # one positional per field update.
+        set_clauses = ["status = $1", "updated_at = now()"]
+        params: list[Any] = [str(new), str(source_id), str(expected)]
+        for col, value in field_updates.items():
+            if col == "metadata":
+                params.append(json.dumps(value or {}))
+                set_clauses.append(f"{col} = ${len(params)}::jsonb")
+            else:
+                params.append(value)
+                set_clauses.append(f"{col} = ${len(params)}")
+
+        sql = f"""
+            UPDATE knowledge_sources
+               SET {', '.join(set_clauses)}
+             WHERE id = $2::uuid
+               AND status = $3
+               AND deleted_at IS NULL
+            RETURNING {_ALL_COLUMNS}
+        """
+        rows = await self.db.query(sql, params)
+        if not rows:
+            return None
+        return _row_to_source(rows[0])
+
+    async def async_count_since(
+        self,
+        business_id: ULID,
+        since: datetime,
+        *,
+        include_deleted: bool = True,
+    ) -> int:
+        """Count rows for `business_id` with `created_at >= since`.
+
+        Defaults to including soft-deleted rows so quota counting can't be
+        bypassed by deleting and re-uploading.
+        """
+        sql = """
+            SELECT COUNT(*) AS n
+              FROM knowledge_sources
+             WHERE business_id = $1::uuid
+               AND created_at >= $2
+        """
+        params: list[Any] = [str(business_id), since]
+        if not include_deleted:
+            sql += " AND deleted_at IS NULL"
+        rows = await self.db.query(sql, params)
+        return int(rows[0]["n"]) if rows else 0
+
+    async def async_soft_delete(
+        self,
+        source_id: ULID,
+        business_id: ULID,
+    ) -> None:
+        """Soft-delete from any non-deleted state (§4.1: any → deleted).
+
+        No-op if the row is already deleted or doesn't belong to this tenant.
+        """
+        now = datetime.utcnow()
+        sql = """
+            UPDATE knowledge_sources
+               SET status = $1,
+                   deleted_at = $2,
+                   updated_at = $2
+             WHERE id = $3::uuid
+               AND business_id = $4::uuid
+               AND deleted_at IS NULL
+        """
+        await self.db.execute(
+            sql,
+            str(KnowledgeSourceStatus.DELETED),
+            now,
+            str(source_id),
+            str(business_id),
+        )
+
+
+__all__ = [
+    "IllegalTransitionError",
+    "KnowledgeSourceRepository",
+]
